entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: Carbon stored in soil, specifically in neutral-to-alkaline soils of the Indo-Gangetic Plain

entities.name: NEUTRAL-TO-ALKALINE SOILS
entities.description: Soils with a pH range that is neutral to alkaline, affecting carbon stability

entities.name: INDO-GANGETIC PLAIN
entities.description: A vast agricultural area in South Asia where the study was conducted

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: Organic components of soil, including fractions and molecular composition

entities.name: SOM FRACTIONATION
entities.description: A technique used to separate soil organic matter into different fractions

entities.name: SOIL INCUBATION
entities.description: A 196-day process used to study carbon respiration and pool kinetics

entities.name: PYROLYSIS-GC/MS
entities.description: An analytical method used to determine the molecular composition of SOM

entities.name: THREE-POOL KINETIC MODEL
entities.description: A model used to estimate labile, intermediate, and stable SOC pools

entities.name: LABILE SOC POOL
entities.description: A fast-turning-over pool of soil organic carbon

entities.name: INTERMEDIATE SOC POOL
entities.description: A pool of soil organic carbon that regulates turnover over decades

entities.name: STABLE SOC POOL
entities.description: A long-term pool of soil organic carbon

entities.name: NON-HYDROLYZABLE SOC
entities.description: The specific component of SOC that constitutes the stable pool

entities.name: PH
entities.description: The acidity or alkalinity of the soil, which influences microbial activity and Al/Fe formation

entities.name: ACTIVE AL/FE
entities.description: Oxalate-extractable aluminum and iron that control the stability of the intermediate SOC pool

entities.name: MINERAL-ASSOCIATED SOM
entities.description: Soil organic matter associated with minerals, closely linked to the intermediate SOC pool

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: Human-led farming practices that affect carbon fractions in topsoil

entities.name: LIGHT FRACTION CARBON
entities.description: A fraction of SOC reduced by agricultural activity in topsoil

entities.name: MICROBIAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: Biological processes in soil driven by pH that contribute to SOM degradation

entities.name: DRIER CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental state that leads to low active Al/Fe levels and SOM degradation

entities.name: CULTIVATION
entities.description: The act of preparing and using land for crops, exacerbating SOC loss in topsoil
